column13: description
DHW scenario member identifier.
Wave scenario member identifier.
Cyclone mortality scenario identifier.
Choice of MCDA approach.
Number of Tabular Acropora to seed per deployment event.
Number of Corymbose Acropora to seed per deployment event.
Number of Corymbose non-Acropora to seed per deployment event.
Number of small massives/encrusting to seed per deployment event.
Number of large massives/encrusting to seed per deployment event.
Number of moving coral settlers added per deployment event.
Number of seeding devices per m².
Minimum number of locations to perform intervention
Minimum number of locations to perform moving corals intervention
Assumed reduction in bleaching mortality.
Reduction in DHWs due to shading.
Assisted adaptation in terms of DHW resistance.
Distance from current year used as referece for assisted adaptation.
Number of years to seed for.
Number of years to shade for.
Number of years to fog for.
How many years of projected data to take into account when selecting intervention locations (0 only accounts for current deployment year).
Frequency of seeding deployments (0 deploys once).
Frequency of fogging deployments (0 deploys once).
Frequency of shading deployments.
Frequency of moving corals deployments.
Start seeding deployments after this number of years has elapsed.
Start of shading deployments after this number of years has elapsed.
Start of fogging deployments after this number of years has elapsed.
Start moving corals deployments after this number of years has elapsed.
Number of years to deploy moving corals.
Albedo level to use from 5D DHW dataset.
Duration level (yearly days) to use from 5D DHW dataset.
Frequency of MCB deployment in years (e.g. 1 is every year, 2 is every 2nd year).
Deployment strategy: 1=Periodic (time-based), 2=Reactive (condition-based); 0 is off
Deployment strategy: 1=Periodic (time-based), 2=Reactive (condition-based); 0 is off
Deployment strategy: 1=Periodic (time-based), 2=Reactive (condition-based); 0 is off
Deploy when coral cover falls below this proportion (for reactive strategy)
Deploy when proportional cover loss exceeds this value (for reactive strategy)
Do not deploy to locations with less than this cover proportion (for reactive strategy)
Timesteps to wait after trigger before deployment (for reactive strategy)
Timesteps before location becomes eligible again; 0=no cooldown (for reactive strategy)
Importance of avoiding heat stress when seeding. Prefer locations with lower heat stress.
Prefer locations with higher wave activity.
Give preference to locations with high incoming connectivity (i.e., receives larvae from other sites) for coral deployments.
Give preference to locations with high outgoing connectivity (i.e., provides larvae to other sites) for coral deployments.
Give preference to deeper locations for coral deployments.
Preference locations with lower coral cover (higher available space) for seeding deployments.
Prefer locations from clusters that are under-represented.
Prefer locations that are distant (when maximized) or closer (when minimized; the default) to their neighbors.
Preference locations with lower heat stress for fogging.
Preference locations with lower wave activity for fogging.
Give preference to locations with high incoming connectivity (i.e., receives larvae from other sites) for fogging deployments.
Give preference to locations with high outgoing connectivity (i.e., provides larvae to other sites) for fogging deployments.
Give preference to shallower locations for fogging deployments.
Higher values give preference to sites with high coral cover for fogging deployments.
Prefer to fog locations in clusters that are under-represented.
Fog locations that are distant (when maximized) or closer (when minimized) to their neighbors.
Importance of avoiding heat stress. Prefer locations with lower heat stress.
Prefer locations with higher wave activity.
Give preference to locations with high incoming connectivity (i.e., receives larvae from other sites) for coral deployments.
Give preference to locations with high outgoing connectivity (i.e., provides larvae to other sites) for coral deployments.
Give preference to deeper locations for coral deployments.
Preference locations with lower coral cover (higher available space).
Prefer locations from clusters that are under-represented.
Prefer locations that are distant (when maximized) or closer (when minimized; the default) to their neighbors.

Minimum depth for a site to be included for consideration.
Note: This value will be replaced with the shallowest depth value found if all sites are found to be deeper than `depth_min + depth_offset`.
Offset from minimum depth, used to indicate maximum depth.
Heritability of DHW tolerance.
